ref: charge: proceed round-2 sync-and-land; HOLD at merge gate (operator-go merge only)
msg: ACK prg17r2-001 — primed/activated, GO. Proceed with the round-2 sync-and-land per round-2-tracker.md. Reaffirm the load-bearing constraints: (1) MERGE origin/main into new-llm, NEVER rebase; throwaway worktree .worktrees/new-llm-sync off origin/new-llm (worktree-setup skill); compute SHAs at runtime. (2) recaps.ts is the ONE conflict AND the one judgment call — does new-llm's new profileSessionToRecapItem/listProfileSessions path need main's WI-821 identityV2Enabled threading (getChildSessions)? resolve with the merge in hand + record your call in outbox. (3) flag-on integration lane is ic-116 allowed-red: DIFF the red SET vs the quota baseline and read per-test PASS in the RIGHT step (colon=cross-pkg, dash=co-located) — a NEW red beyond baseline = real fail. (4) WI-819 is ALREADY Closed/Done against new-llm — touch NO Cosmo WI; this landing closes nothing (git-level done-signal only). (5) MERGE GATE: required CI green + claude-review COMMENT 0-must/0-should, then STOP and surface to me via outbox — the final new-llm->main --no-ff merge is OPERATOR-GO ONLY, do NOT self-merge to main. Step 0: confirm origin/new-llm actually contains PR #1231 (WI-819) before merging main in. Next checkpoint I want: worktree+merge set up and recaps.ts resolved (with your semantic call).

ACK + verified independently: origin/main IS a full ancestor of origin/new-llm (0 behind / 44 ahead), recaps.ts +66/-0 (zero conflict), identityV2Enabled threading confirmed in-tree (new path forwards opts?.identityV2Enabled). CONCUR with skipping the no-op merge — do NOT run it for an audit artifact: main is already an ancestor, so `git merge origin/main` is already-up-to-date => produces NO commit => there is nothing to record. Proceed: drive PR #1232 to the merge gate. On the flag-on integration lane apply ic-116 discrimination (diff red SET vs quota baseline; per-test PASS in the right step). Surface at merge-ready with the claude-review COMMENT verdict (0-must/0-should or each dispositioned) + the required-CI status (main / API Quality Gate / Playwright web smoke / Merge completeness check); then HOLD for operator --no-ff go. Do NOT self-merge.

ref: prg17r2-003 — PROCESS CORRECTION (supersedes the weaker validation framing in orch-001/002 + the round-2 tracker)
msg: Your CI triage was good (ic-116 flag-on discrimination 48==48 comm -13 empty vs main baseline 27772532944 ACCEPTED; GC1 correctly isolated as the one real red). BUT the PROCESS so far skipped standard executor rigor — that is a defect in my brief, now corrected: executor-protocol.md is BINDING for this round and YOU are the executor it governs (not just hypothetical sub-executors). Per its Phases 4-6, before #1232 may be called merge-ready you MUST: (1) do ALL validation LOCALLY in your worktree off origin/new-llm — NOT deferred to PR CI (operator ruling: deferring local checks to CI is NOT acceptable). Local gate: tsc --build; affected jest incl. the fixed apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-navigation-contract.test.tsx; pnpm eval:llm (Tier 1); the non-flag-gated spot-checks (S0-R gate, consent/monthly-report-v2); GC1 script (scripts/check-gc1-pattern-a.ts) green locally. (2) MANDATORY Codex adversarial review (Phase 4) — a FRESH session / NO inherited context, NOT a fork (use codex:codex-rescue or an equiv fresh adversarial reviewer), capped 3 iterations, over (a) your round-2 diff and (b) the landing's base=main-only risk surface — the GC1 red IS that class (base=new-llm per-WI review can't see base=main ratchets), so hunt for SIBLING base=main-only failures, not just GC1. Fix valid findings; record rejections with rationale. (3) Then bring #1232 to the STRICT green-PR gate (every required check SUCCESS, claude-review actually green, mergeStateStatus CLEAN). GC1 fix: prefer Pattern-A requireActual where the hook runs in unit env; gc1-allow with a truthful reason is acceptable (round-1 WI-695 precedent). Authority ENDS at the green-PR report: do NOT merge, do NOT self-waive any required red — HOLD for operator --no-ff go. Report at green-PR (WITH the Codex adversarial-review summary + local-validation evidence) or at blocked/3-round-residual.

ref: MISSION CHANGE — HALT inline fixing; switch to AUDIT+CATALOG (operator ruling)
msg: STOP — operator ruling. Do NOT fix anything inline. Halt the GC1 fix and push NOTHING to new-llm (no commits, no PR updates, no merges). MISSION CHANGE: you are now an AUDITOR, not a lander. Test, review, and audit new-llm; for EVERY issue you find — the GC1 base=main ratchet, the flag-on POST /v1/profiles 500s, ANY other base=main-only red on #1232, and canon-fit gaps in the V2-shell/LLM deltas — DRAFT a catalog entry (title, concrete repro, one-line suggested fix, severity, suspected owner ours-vs-Zuzka) and MOVE ON. Fix NONE of them. Your Codex adversarial review (executor-protocol Phase 4 rigor) STAYS, but now as the AUDIT ENGINE — its output is catalog entries, not edits. Keep PR #1232 OPEN as the base=main CI oracle but treat it DO-NOT-MERGE: do not merge, do not close. The new-llm->main LANDING is DEFERRED to a future round (it will be blocked on the catalog clearing). For the GC1 fix you already drafted: do NOT push it — fold it into its catalog entry, noting the fix is trivial (Pattern-A requireActual or gc1-allow, round-1 WI-695 precedent) so a later executor is fast. BOUND the audit: one actionable entry per finding, do NOT root-cause to the bottom of each (that is the never-ending thread we are avoiding). Do NOT create the Cosmo WIs yet — REPORT the drafted catalog to me first; I will route WI-home + ownership, then greenlight creation. Acknowledge the mission change in your next outbox line.

(1) WORK ALREADY PRODUCED — leave it: the staged-only GC1 fix in your worktree is the captured suggested-fix artifact for its catalog entry; do NOT push, do NOT discard. GUARDRAIL: keep your audit BASELINE = true origin/new-llm — park/stash that staged GC1 edit while auditing so re-running the GC1 check still reproduces the red; do NOT let your own staged fix mask the finding. PR #1232 stays OPEN as the base=main CI oracle. (2) CODEX REVIEW (running): when it returns, it is a CATALOG SOURCE, not a fix trigger. TRIAGE each finding — valid / duplicate-of-existing / false-positive, + severity + suspected owner ours-vs-Zuzka — then add valid+unique ones as catalog entries, and record false-positives as entries MARKED invalid+rationale so they are not re-raised. Fix NONE. Treat that review as ONE audit source, not the whole audit: your dimension passes continue — (i) main-readiness (every base=main-only red on #1232 + why), (ii) canon-fit of the V2-shell/LLM deltas, (iii) functional smoke of headline changes. (3) SUPERSEDE orch-003: the executor-protocol RIGOR (adversarial discipline, verify, no rogue inline decisions) still binds, but its FIX/MERGE phases (4 fix-loop, 5/6 green-PR, 7 complete) DO NOT apply in audit mode — your output is the catalog + report to me, not a green PR. Still: report the drafted catalog before any Cosmo WI creation.

ref: INCIDENT ruling: ACCEPT (A) the FF state — NO revert/force-push; gate the land on #1232 strict-green; close #1233; fix-forward the bare-push root cause
msg: Ruling: (A) ACCEPT — do NOT revert, NO force-push on new-llm. Independently verified: origin/new-llm=ddd9beef2 (the 3 intended green-up commits, FF-only, no rewrite), origin/main UNCHANGED (ddc549927), #1232 head now=ddd9beef2 so the land PR (claude-review + all required checks) gates exactly this content. RATIONALE: the work is fully local-validated + FRESH-session adversarially-reviewed (0 findings); the only thing bypassed was the intermediate #1233 ceremony whose end-state == what's on new-llm now; the protected boundary (main/prod) is untouched and the #1232 land gate is intact. Reverting = a destructive shared-branch force-push to restore an identical end-state — not justified. DO NOW: (1) CLOSE PR #1233 as superseded (head new-llm-r2-gc1 is behind new-llm; comment: content fast-forwarded onto new-llm via ddd9beef2, gated at #1232). (2) WATCH new-llm's own CI + #1232 to STRICT green (all required SUCCESS + claude-review COMMENT verdict 0must/0should + allowed-red lanes DIFFED vs baseline); report strict-green OR any NEW required red (vs the C2/ic-116 baseline). (3) CONFIRM #1232 is mergeable-not-CONFLICTING — main is now 1 ahead via the WI-805 squash; if it CONFLICTS, merge origin/main INTO new-llm in your worktree, resolve, and push with an EXPLICIT refspec (NOT bare). (4) once new-llm CI green + #1232 strict-green, FINALIZE WI-842/843/844 → Reviewing (reviewer closes; they're delivered on new-llm + validated). THEN I do the new-llm->main --no-ff LAND (executors never merge to main). ROOT CAUSE (fix-forward, not your fault to fix tonight): forked /commit ran a BARE `git push` against a worktree whose upstream = origin/new-llm → direct FF. RULE for the rest of this lane: ALWAYS push with an explicit refspec `HEAD:<wi-branch>`, never bare, in any worktree tracking origin/new-llm. I'm capturing this as a commit-skill/worktree-setup hardening item. HARD RULES intact: nothing to main/WI-805/prod/drop by you.

FIX each chain item the ratchet's INTENDED way (e.g. recaps.ts inline requireProfileId → withProfile(c)) OR a JUSTIFIED baseline bump with a documented rationale comment — each fix minimal/mechanical. STOP-AND-REPORT condition (role-clean guardrail): any chain item that is NOT a trivial ratchet-conformance fix — a real new-llm logic bug, a behavior change, or anything needing design judgment — STOP, route it to its own WI, report; do NOT self-expand into substantive scope. TRACEABILITY: open ONE consolidated WI 'new-llm: clear base=main ratchet trips for the land' (WS-13/PRG-17, Task/P2) listing the enumerated chain (C7 + any siblings); per-ratchet WIs only if one turns non-trivial. Then finalize WI-842/843/844 + the new ratchet WI → Reviewing; report. I do the --no-ff LAND. Nothing to main/WI-805/prod/drop by you.

ref: GRANT autonomous push-window authority (no manual signal needed) with collision guardrails + stop-condition; then I land on strict-green
msg: Excellent work — fixes + V2 regression test + the dead-route correction are exactly right. You do NOT need to wait for a manual window signal from me (operator mandate = autonomous execution). GRANTED: self-determine the push window with these guardrails: (1) Push your prepared fixes (rebase 6273f98ef onto the settled new-llm tip → EXPLICIT refspec HEAD:new-llm, never bare, never force) ONLY when BOTH: (a) new-llm tip has been STABLE ≥30 min (no new WI-825 commits), AND (b) the PR's WI-825 must-fixes (MF1/SF1/SF2 + the C2/Flag-ON set) show cleared/converging on claude-review — i.e. you're not pushing into active churn. (2) Additive push only — if the remote moved between fetch and push (non-FF reject), ABORT, re-fetch, re-evaluate; never force. Your commits only ADD on top, never disturb WI-825's. (3) After push, drive claude-review to 0must/0should + confirm all required SUCCESS + allowed-red DIFFED vs the settled baseline; then REPORT strict-green. (4) STOP-CONDITION: if new-llm won't settle (WI-825 keeps moving) by ~2-3h or you're churning re-reviews, STOP + report for operator cross-lane sequencing in the AM — do NOT churn indefinitely. (5) On your strict-green report I do the new-llm→main --no-ff LAND (I merge to main; you never do). Then finalize WI-842/843/844/845/846 → Reviewing. Keep MF1/SF1/SF2/C2 untouched (WI-825/817/805). ref: INVESTIGATE the new XP-sync removal (intentional vs regression) via sub-agent — your new-llm-correctness charter; land gates on REAL strict-green not the nondeterministic MF=0
msg: Your lane is confirmed fully clean + SF4 self-cleared (comment worked) — exception moot, good. Agreed: MF=0 is reviewer nondeterminism (apply-retention unchanged, Flag-ON still red) — I will NOT land on it; real strict-green only. NEW XP-SYNC FINDING → INVESTIGATE (this is squarely your original new-llm-correctness AUDITOR charter; delegate to a SUB-AGENT to keep your context lean): is the removal of `syncXpLedgerStatus` from processRecallTest (retention-data.ts) + handleReviewCalibrationGrade (inngest/functions/review-calibration-grade.ts) INTENTIONAL (XP-ledger sync moved/superseded elsewhere in new-llm) or a REAL REGRESSION (users stop getting XP credit for recall sessions + calibration grades)? Trace whether XP sync still happens on those paths in new-llm.   • If INTENTIONAL/superseded → document the justification (cite where XP sync now occurs); that clears the should-fix.   • If REAL REGRESSION → do NOT fix inline (real bug, proper treatment): CREATE a WI in WS-13/PRG-17 (new-llm correctness, Type=Bug, severity per impact — note prod is pre-launch so 0 current users, but it'd bite at launch), report it, and I route the fix. Either way it must resolve BEFORE I land (don't ship a silent XP regression to main). LAND GATE (real strict-green): required all SUCCESS + claude-review 0must/0-VALID-should STABLE (not a one-shot nondeterministic snapshot) + Flag-ON allowed-red DIFFED vs baseline + XP-sync resolved + WS-18/847 pushed (apply-retention fix → Flag-ON green + their identity-graph/duck-type should-fixes). WS-18/847 is inbound (shepherd started it). Continue HOLDING; report the XP-sync verdict + strict-green. Nothing to main/WI-805/prod/drop by you.

After this, your items are fully exhausted. LAND GATE remaining: WS-18/847 push (apply-retention → Flag-ON green + identity-graph/duck-type should-fixes) + claude-review STABLE 0must/0-valid-should + required all SUCCESS + Flag-ON allowed-red DIFFED vs baseline. msg: Two things (operator-directed): (1) REVIEWS ARE RUNNING NOW on your 5 finalized WIs — the operator just pointed the reviewer at WS-13/PRG-17 (it wasn't pulling this workstream before). ARM a review-results MONITOR: a Cosmo-Stage watcher on WI-842/843/844/845/846 for the Gate-2 verdicts — Reviewing→Closed = pass; Reviewing→Executing = BOUNCE. On a bounce, handle it promptly (read the reviewer's reasons, fix or rebut from source per your usual rigor, re-finalize). Don't go fully dark until all 5 close. (2) KICK OFF WI-848 NOW (operator green-lit — no reason to defer; code is on main, self-contained, no deps, and you have the freshest context having found it). CLAIM WI-848, fresh worktree off origin/MAIN (.worktrees/WI-848, per worktree-setup — the code landed, so base=main now), and DELEGATE the fix to a SUB-AGENT under full rigor (keep your context lean; write it a precise brief from your investigation): the decay case — on xpChange==='decayed', applyRetentionUpdate (retention-data.ts:887) sets retention_cards.xpStatus='decayed' but does NOT update xp_ledger.status, so progress.ts (~374/466) reads a stale 'verified'. FIX = targeted xp_ledger.status write for the decay case (mirror the verified-at-insert invariant) + RED-GREEN test (write the failing test first, watch it fail, fix, watch it pass). FRESH-session adversarial review. PR base=main; drive to strict-green; I merge on green (executors never merge to main) OR you report green and I land. Run (1) and (2) in parallel — the monitor catches bounces while the sub-agent builds WI-848. Push explicit-refspec only; nothing else to main without my merge.
